coredata.dc:description: Ofloxacin (OFL) is an effective antibiotic against bacteria. However, the overuse of ofloxacin can lead to residues in animal-derived foods, posing a potential threat to human health. Due to the issue of false positives in single-mode detection, it is crucial to establish a multi-mode method for accurate and sensitive detection of ofloxacin. Peroxidase-like active iron-nickel bimetallic organic framework (FeNi-MOF) and orange fluorescent carbon dots (OCDs) synthesized by a simple one-pot method were used to establish a dual-mode colorimetric/ratiometric fluorescence biosensor for the detection of ofloxacin residues. In colorimetric mode, in the presence of hydrogen peroxide (H2O2), FeNi-MOF can catalyze the oxidation of 3,3′,5,5′-tetramethylbenzidine (TMB) to produce blue oxTMB. However, the fluorescence of OCDs at 588 nm will be quenched by oxTMB, thus forming a ratiometric fluorescence signal with the fluorescence of FeNi-MOF at 420 nm. The linear range of the dual-mode detection method was 10−7-10−2 mg/mL, and the detection limits were 1.1 ng/kg (colorimetric) and 0.34 ng/kg (radiometric fluorescence). In addition, the constructed smartphone sensing platform is also well applied for on-site portable detection. Therefore, we have established a dual-mode sensor with high specificity, accuracy, and excellent anti-interference ability for OFL detection, and satisfactory results are also obtained in the actual spiked samples.

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Ofloxacin (OFL) is an effective antibiotic against bacteria. However, the overuse of ofloxacin can lead to residues in animal-derived foods, posing a potential threat to human health. Due to the issue of false positives in single-mode detection, it is crucial to establish a multi-mode method for accurate and sensitive detection of ofloxacin. Peroxidase-like active iron-nickel bimetallic organic framework (FeNi-MOF) and orange fluorescent carbon dots (OCDs) synthesized by a simple one-pot method were used to establish a dual-mode colorimetric/ratiometric fluorescence biosensor for the detection of ofloxacin residues. In colorimetric mode, in the presence of hydrogen peroxide (H2O2), FeNi-MOF can catalyze the oxidation of 3,3′,5,5′-tetramethylbenzidine (TMB) to produce blue oxTMB. However, the fluorescence of OCDs at 588 nm will be quenched by oxTMB, thus forming a ratiometric fluorescence signal with the fluorescence of FeNi-MOF at 420 nm. The linear range of the dual-mode detection method was 10−7-10−2 mg/mL, and the detection limits were 1.1 ng/kg (colorimetric) and 0.34 ng/kg (radiometric fluorescence). In addition, the constructed smartphone sensing platform is also well applied for on-site portable detection. Therefore, we have established a dual-mode sensor with high specificity, accuracy, and excellent anti-interference ability for OFL detection, and satisfactory results are also obtained in the actual spiked samples.

ce:data-availability.ce:para: Data will be made available on request.

ce:section.ce:para: Quinolones are widely used in aquaculture (Bondad-Reantaso et al., 2023), animal husbandry (He et al., 2020), and human clinical medicine (Zeng et al., 2022) as a class of synthetic antibacterial drugs. They can disrupt topoisomerase IV and DNA gyrase to inhibit bacterial nucleic acid synthesis, causing bacterial chromosome breakage (Pazda, Kumirska, Stepnowski, & Mulkiewicz, 2019). Ofloxacin (OFL) is one of the most widely used third-generation quinolone antibiotics (Deng et al., 2022). That has a good antibacterial effect on gram bacteria such as Shigella, Proteus, and Helicobacter pylori (Song et al., 2020). In order to protect the health of consumers, the European Union (EU) and the U.S. Food and Drug Administration stipulates that the residue of fluoroquinolones in animal-derived foods is 30–1900 μg/kg. In pursuit of economic benefits, some farmers disregard the prohibition period for drugs during the aquaculture process, leading to the misuse and abuse of antibiotics. This results in excessive residues of these drugs in aquatic products. Humans can ingest antibiotics through the food chain, then enrich and amplify, adversely affecting the human body (Pan et al., 2022). In the national standard of the Maximum Residue Limits of 41 veterinary drugs in food of the National Food Safety Standard (GB31650.1–2022) issued in 2022, the maximum residue limit of OFL is 2 μg/kg in the liver and muscle of aquatic products, edible animals such as poultry and livestock, and the daily allowable intake is 0–5 μg/kg. Therefore, it is necessary to establish an accurate, efficient, and specific detection platform for detecting OFL residues.

To date, the reported methods of OFL detection mainly include high-performance liquid chromatography (HPLC) (Elgendy, Zaky, Alaa Eldin, & Fadel, 2023), liquid chromatography-mass spectrometry (LC-MS) (Maia, Paíga, Delerue-Matos, Castro, & Tiritan, 2020), and electrochemical methods (Mozhgan, Shohreh, Mehran, & Mehdi, 2022). Although these methods have high sensitivity, specificity, and a wide application range, they require more expensive instruments (Murugavelu, Sharma, Devaraj, Huanhuan, & Quansheng, 2021), high requirements for technicians (Wu et al., 2021), and the stability of materials needs to be improved (Y. Li, Han, Yu, Jiang, & Luo, 2024). Some methods require materials with complex structures and difficult preparation, which makes achieving real-time rapid detection conditions difficult. Biosensors for dual-mode detection are being developed to address these issues. Such as, Wang et al., 2023. constructed a biosensor to detect pesticide residues in vegetables using a colorimetric/electrochemical dual-mode (G. Wang et al., 2024c). Dual-mode detection improves the sensitivity and accuracy of detection. Li et al. developed a dual-mode colorimetric/fluorescent aptamer sensor for the detection of aflatoxin B1, which not only has a low detection limit but also demonstrates satisfactory performance in practical applications (S. Li et al., 2023).

Metal-organic frameworks (MOFs) are a new class of porous materials with crystalline structures known for their large specific surface area (Hammad et al., 2024), uniform structure (Udaya Rajesh, Mathew, Kumar, Singhal, & Thomas, 2024), and ease of functionalization (X. Zhang et al., 2024). Benefiting from large specific surface area and high porosity, MOFs have attracted significant interest from researchers in drug-delivery (Kareem et al., 2022), catalysis, and hydrogen storage (Razavi et al., 2024). Functionalized MOFs also have diverse applications in various fields. For example, when the surface is modified with enzymes, antibodies, or aptamers, they can be used to target and identify specific substances. Additionally, when combined with certain coordination compounds, they can exhibit enhanced enzyme-like activity or be endowed with fluorescence properties. In summary, MOFs are widely used and show great potential as candidate materials for constructing biosensors. Carbon dots (CDs) are a type of carbon nanomaterial with promising applications in bioimaging (F. Wang et al., 2024a), biosensors (Zuo et al., 2023), and food and drug analysis (W. Wang et al., 2024b; Yin et al., 2024)due to their low toxicity, cost-effectiveness, good biocompatibility, and high sensitivity (Guo et al., 2024; Rui et al., 2023; Tran et al., 2023). In particular, their fluorescence properties have been greatly applied in constructing ratiometric fluorescence signals for detection (Kayani et al., 2024; L. Yang et al., 2024b; S. Yang et al., 2024a).

In this experiment, a biosensor combining MOF and CDs was established for dual-mode detection of OFL. The concentration change trend of OFL and FeNi-MOF@Apt is opposite. FeNi-MOF@Apt with peroxidase-like activity catalyzes H2O2 to trigger a Fenton-like effect, producing •OH, which oxidizes colorless TMB into blue oxTMB (Boruah & Das, 2020), thereby generating colorimetric signals. On the other hand, the fluorescence intensity of FeNi-MOF@Apt at 420 nm increases with concentration, while the fluorescence intensity of OCDs at 588 nm is quenched by oxTMB through the internal filter effect (IFE) (Guoliang et al., 2016), resulting in a ratio fluorescence signal. Additionally, the dual-mode color parameters are obtained by imaging through the smartphone sensing platform and then using the color recognition application (APP). Finally, the ratio of RGB (The color parameters of Red, Green and blue; Green/Blue, Red/Blue) was calculated, and the standard curve was established to realize on-site detection of OFL. In the detection process, a combination of colorimetric and fluorescence detection modes was used. While considering the advantages of both methods, it is more important that the obtained results can be mutually confirmed. This method not only overcomes the interference of coexisting substances to the greatest extent but also utilizes the dual-mode self-calibration function to minimize errors caused by environmental factors and different experimental equipment, thereby preventing the occurrence of false positive/negative events in the conventional method of OFL detection.

Sodium chloride (NaCl), Lead chloride (PbCl2), Ultrapure water (resistance value ≥ 18.2 mΩ⋅cm) Furazolidone (FZD), Sulfapyridine (SPD), Ofloxacin (OFL), Ciprofloxacin (CIP), Norfloxacin (NOR), Enrofloxacin (ENR), 2-Aminoterephthalic acid (NH2-BDC), N-Hydroxy succinimide (NHS), Potassium chloride (KCl), Hydrogen peroxide (H2O2, 30%), 1-(3-Dimethylaminopropyl)-3-ethylcarbodiimide hydrochloride (EDC), N,N-Dimethylformamide (DMF), Ethanol absolute (C2H5OH), Tween-20, Tris (hydroxymethyl) aminomethane (Tris), Hydrochloric acid (HCl), 3,3′,5,5′-tetramethylbenzidine (TMB), Ofloxacin antigen antibody, Ofloxacin aptamer, Sodium acetate (CH3COONa), Acetic acid (CH3COOH), Acetonitrile (C2H3N), Cyclohexane (C6H12), Rhodamine B, Sodium hydroxide (NaOH), Dialysis bag (1000 MW), Horseradish peroxidase (HRP), 96-microwell plates, Ofloxacin aptamer sequence: 5′-COOH-ATA CCA GCT TAT TCA ATT AGT TGT GTA TTG AGG TTT GAT CTA GGC ATA GTC AAC AGA GCA CGA TCG ATC TGG CTT GTT CTA CAA TCG TAA TCA GTTAG-3′ (98-mer).

Scanning electron microscopy (SEM, Hitachi, S-4800, Co., Japan), The wavenumber range of Fourier transform infrared spectroscopy (FT-IR, Shimadzu, IRTracer-100 Co., Japan) is from 400 cm−1 to 4000 cm−1, and the experimental results are recorded by an AKX1703426 spectrometer, X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS, Thermo, EscaLab 250XI, Co., USA), Ultraviolet–visible spectrophotometer (UV–vis, Shimadzu, UV-2550, Co., Japan), Transmission electron microscopy (TEM, FEI Tecnai G2 F30, Co., USA), Fluorescence spectrophotometer (FL, Hitachi High-tech, F-7000, Co., Japan), X-ray diffractometer (XRD, Rigaku Corporation, Ultima Ⅳ Co., Japan), Laser particle size analyzer (Zetasizer Nano ZS90, Malvern Co., UK), The photos were captured by smartphone. The color recognition program can be obtained from the app store.

A simple one-step solvothermal synthesis was conducted with slight modifications based on previous literature (Mu, Wu, Guo, Zhao, & Wang, 2022). Briefly, 0.810 g Ferric chloride hexahydrate (FeCl3·6H2O), 0.238 g Nickel chloride hexahydrate (NiCl2·6H2O), and 0.181 g NH2-BDC were fully dissolved in 20 mL of DMF. Then, 2 mL of NaOH solution (0.4 M) was slowly added drop by drop, stirring for 10 min using a magnetic stirrer. Then the mixture was poured to a Teflon-lined reactor and heated at 100 °C for 8 h. After cooling, centrifuge (10,000 rpm, 15 min) the cooled solution, discard the supernatant. The obtained precipitate was washed with DMF and anhydrous ethanol, and the washing was repeated three times. Then the precipitate was dried in a vacuum drying oven at 60 °C for 5 h to obtain a brown product.

The FeNi-MOF with an amino group was coupled with an aptamer modified with a carboxyl group, which was slightly modified based on previous literature (Zhu et al., 2021). In brief, 1 mL of EDC (1 mg/mL, Tris-HCl buffer as the solvent) and 1 mL of NHS (1 mg/mL, Tris-HCl buffer as the solvent) were added to 3 mL of carboxyl-modified aptamer solution (15 μg/mL). The mixture was shaken for 30 min to activate the carboxyl group, and then 5 mL of FeNi-MOF (20 μg/mL) was added to continue shaking for 3 h. After centrifugation to remove the unconnected aptamer, the obtained FeNi-MOF@Apt was re-dissolved in 5 mL of Tris-HCl buffer solution (pH = 7.4, 10 mM) and stored at 4 °C.

20 mg of rhodamine B was completely dissolved in 10 mL of ultrapure water. The solution is then transferred to a PTFE-lined reactor and heated for 2 h at 160 °C. After natural cooling, the resulting solution was centrifuged at 10,000 rpm for 15 min. The precipitate was discarded, and the supernatant was retained for further treatment. The obtained supernatant was dialyzed overnight in ultrapure water using a dialysis bag (1000 MW) to obtain pure OCDs, and the powder was collected by freeze-drying.

Firstly, 200 μL of 1 × 10−2 mg/mL ofloxacin antibody was added into 96-well microtiter plates. In order to fix the antibody on the well plate, the treated well plate was placed in a 4 °C refrigerator overnight. The well plates, after incubation, were washed three times with a 0.05% Tween-20 Tris-HCl buffer solution (TBST, pH = 7.4, 10 mM) for 5 min each time. In order to prevent non-specific adsorption affecting the experimental results, 200 μL of 1% bovine serum albumin (BSA) blocking solution was added to each well and blocked at 37 °C for 1 h. After washing again, the standard solution containing OFL (antigen) (10−7, 10−6, 10−5, 10−4, 10−3, 10−2 mg/mL) was added into each well, 200 μL per well, incubated at 37 °C for 1 h to coat antigen. The blank control group consisted of Tris-HCl buffer solution (pH = 7.4, 10 mM) without adding antigen. After the antigen-antibody reaction was completed, the washing steps were repeated (the treatment method of the blank control group was the same as above); Finally, the FeNi-MOF@Apt solution was added into a 96-well microplate, with 200 μL per well. The microplate was then incubated at 37 °C for 1 h, and the supernatant after the reaction was dispersed into the HAc-NaAc buffer solution (pH = 4, 0.2 M). Then, 100 μL of 6 mM H2O2 solution were added. All subsequent experiments were carried out in a 2 mL centrifuge tube at room temperature. The sensing system maintained the volume of 2 mL solution, and the reaction time was 10 min. In colorimetric mode, 100 μL of TMB solution with a concentration of 8 mM was added, and the UV absorption spectrum of the reaction system was recorded from 450 nm to 700 nm. In the ratiometric fluorescence mode, 100 μL of 8 mM TMB solution and 100 μL of OCDs solution were added to collect the fluorescence spectrum of the reaction system at the excitation wavelength of 370 nm. The fluorescence intensity at 420 nm and 588 nm was selected to calculate the ratio (F420 nm/F588 nm). When COFL is within 10−7-10−2 mg/mL, both colorimetric and ratiometric fluorescence modes demonstrate a strong linear relationship.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Real sample detection and specificity analysis
ce:section.ce:para: Samples of chicken, prawn, and grass carp were purchased from local markets. After removing skin and bones from the sample, the muscle tissue was taken to make meat emulsion. The 5 g treated sample was homogenized with 20 mL of acetonitrile, centrifuged (5000 rpm, 10 min), and repeatedly extracted twice. The resulting supernatant was combined and diluted to 40 mL. Then, 10 mL of n-hexane was added for static stratification, and the lower acetonitrile layer was retained and evaporated until the powder appeared. The extract was dissolved in 10 mM Tris-HCl buffer (pH = 7.4), and stored at 4 °C. In order to verify the feasibility and accuracy of dual-mode detection, 1 × 10−6, 1 × 10−5, 1 × 10−4 mg/mL of OFL standard was added to the solution obtained after the above treatment as the spiked sample for dual mode determination. In addition, we also conducted a repetitive assessment.

The structural characteristics of FeNi-MOF were analyzed by X-ray diffraction (XRD). As shown in Fig. 1 A, the main diffraction peaks at 9.3°, 10.2°, 16.5°, and 18.6° in the XRD pattern correspond to (002), (101), (103), and (200), which are consistent with the main diffraction peaks shown in previous literature (Ruting et al., 2024), indicating the successful synthesis of Fe and Ni bimetallic organic frameworks. The approximate size and surface morphology of the synthesized FeNi-MOF were observed by scanning electron microscopy (SEM). In Fig. 1B, it is shown that the synthesized FeNi-MOF has a characteristic rhombic structure with a size of about 500 nm. And in Fig. 1B, it can be seen that the FeNi-MOF is uniformly distributed and has good dispersibility. The Fourier transform infrared (FTIR) spectrum of FeNi-MOF are shown in Fig. 1C. The appearance of a relatively wide double peak at 3338–3462 cm−1 is due to the symmetric and asymmetric stretching vibrations of the -OH and -NH2 groups in FeNi-MOF. At the same time, an obvious N-H bond bending vibration appears at 1257 cm−1, which proves the presence of amino groups on the surface of FeNi-MOF. The composition of FeNi-MOF was analyzed by X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS). It can be seen in Fig. 1D that the corresponding elements at 285 eV, 399 eV, 532 eV, 712 eV, and 858 eV are C 1s, N 1s, O 1s, Fe 2p, and Ni 2p, respectively. The presence of Fe and Ni peaks proves that the synthesized metal-organic framework successfully doped these two metal elements. In addition, high-resolution spectral analysis of Fe 2p and Ni 2p was carried out and shown in Fig. 1E and F. In the high-resolution Fe 2p spectrum (Fig. 1E), there are two main peaks of Fe 2p3/2 with a binding energy of 711.4 eV and Fe 2p1/2 with a binding energy of 724.47 eV, along with obvious satellite peaks, indicating that Fe exists in the trivalent form (L. Wang et al., 2024c). As shown in Fig. 1F, two peaks with binding energies of 855.5 eV and 873.2 eV appear in the high-resolution spectrum of Ni 2p, and the accompanying two satellite peaks. This is due to the existence of Ni 2p3/2 and Ni 2p1/2. Besides, the splitting of the two main peaks indicates that Ni exists in the divalent state in the synthesized metal-organic framework (Han et al., 2023).

OCDs were characterized by TEM. The HRTEM images in Fig. 1G clearly show the crystalline characteristics of OCDs with a lattice fringe spacing of 0.21 nm. Fig. 1H shows that the shape of OCDs is close to the spherical average particle size of 4.3 ± 0.02 nm and exhibits good dispersion. In Fig. 1I, it can also be observed that the synthesized OCDs exhibit typical excitation-independent emission behavior. These results demonstrated that OCDs were successfully synthesized.

For demonstrating that the aptamer was successfully linked to FeNi-MOF, we used zeta potential (Y. Zhang et al., 2023), UV spectrophotometry (Ouyang et al., 2021), and SEM for characterization (Liang et al., 2023). In Fig. S1A, the potential of the aptamer was −14.7 mV, indicating that the aptamer carried a negative charge. The potential of the FeNi-MOF was +27.8 mV, indicating that the charge of the FeNi-MOF was positive, and the potential of the FeNi-MOF@Apt was −5.3 mV. The change in potential can be attributed to the attachment of the aptamer to the FeNi-MOF. The absorption spectra of FeNi-MOF@Apt and FeNi-MOF were compared using ultraviolet spectrophotometer. As shown in Fig. S1B, the characteristic peak of the single aptamer was observed at 260 nm. The UV absorption spectra of FeNi-MOF@Apt and FeNi-MOF show a high degree of consistency from 300 to 600 nm; however, the absorbance of the aptamer-labeled FeNi-MOF (FeNi-MOF@Apt) significantly increased at 260 nm, indicating the successful modification of the aptamer on FeNi-MOF. Furthermore, the SEM image of the prepared FeNi-MOF@Apt is shown in Fig. S1C. The shape of FeNi-MOF remained relatively intact before and after aptamer modification, but the surface of FeNi-MOF became rough after the aptamer modification (Fig. S1D), which further proves the successful modification of the aptamer.

To confirm the synthesized MOF's peroxidase-like activity compared to natural enzymes, we conducted enzymatic reaction kinetics experiments using natural horseradish peroxidase (HRP) as a reference (Table S1). In a 2 mL centrifuge tube, 100 μL of H2O2 and TMB with concentrations of 1–5 mM were added, respectively, to FeNi-MOF, FeNi-MOF@Apt, and HRP with the same concentration, and let them react for 10 min (A buffer solution with a pH of 4 was added to ensure final volume of 2 mL). The absorbance value was measured by an ultraviolet spectrophotometer at 652 nm and then calculated. The calculation method is based on the Beer-Lambert Law and the Michaelis-Menten equation.

V0: Initial rate of the reaction, Vmax: Maximum velocity, Km: Michaelis constant, and [S]: Substrate concentration.

From Table S1, it is evident that the Michaelis constant (Km) values of nanomaterials with H2O2 or TMB as substrates are smaller than those of natural horseradish peroxidase (HRP), indicating that the synthesized MOF exhibits superior substrate affinity compared to natural peroxidase enzymes. This is because the Km value can reflect the affinity of the enzyme to the substrate. The lower the Km value, the higher the affinity. The enzymatic reaction is also easier to obtain the maximum reaction velocity (Vmax). This also signifies higher enzyme activity. Furthermore, the maximum reaction velocity of FeNi-MOF@Apt is higher than that of FeNi-MOF, possibly due to aptamer modification enhancing the catalytic activity of nanozymes (Esmaelpourfarkhani, Ramezani, Alibolandi, Abnous, & Taghdisi, 2023).

ce:section.ce:section-title: Mechanism study of OFL dual-mode detection
ce:section.ce:para: Scheme 1 illustrates the biosensor's mechanism in the detection process. With the increase in the content of OFL adsorbed on the 96-well cell culture plate, the content of the sandwich complex (Antibody-Antigen-FeNi-MOF@Apt) gradually increased, while the content of FeNi-MOF@Apt in the supernatant gradually decreased. Based on this, we used the supernatant after the reaction for subsequent experiments. In the process of colorimetric mode detection, FeNi-MOF@Apt with peroxidase-like activity catalyzes H2O2 to produce •OH, oxidizing TMB to oxTMB. The blue color gradually lightens with the increase in OFL content absorbed onto the well plate. With the increase in OFL concentration, the absorption peak recorded by the UV spectrophotometer at 652 nm also showed a decreasing trend. When using ratiometric fluorescence mode detection, as the content of OFL adsorbed on the microplate increases, there is a corresponding decrease in the content of FeNi-MOF@Apt in the supernatant. This leads to two results. Firstly, the decrease in the content of FeNi-MOF@Apt with fluorescence properties leads to a decrease in the fluorescence intensity at 420 nm. Secondly, the low concentration of FeNi-MOF@Apt catalyzed oxTMB will be reduced, and the quenching effect on OCDs will be weakened, resulting in a higher fluorescence intensity at 588 nm. Therefore, the fluorescence ratio at F420 nm/F588 nm gradually decreases, resulting in a ratiometric fluorescence signal.

Fig. 2 A shows that FeNi-MOF and H2O2 produce •OH through Fenton-like effect (Chenyang, Yuxin, & FuGen, 2021), which in turn oxidizes TMB to blue oxTMB, resulting in a significantly higher absorbance at 652 nm than other reaction systems. It is proved that the synthesized FeNi-MOF has good peroxidase-like activity. The specific process can be expressed as:

It can be observed in Fig. 2B that after the addition of oxTMB, the fluorescence of OCDs at 588 nm was significantly quenched. As shown in Fig. 2C, because the ultraviolet absorption spectrum of oxTMB overlaps with the fluorescence emission spectrum of OCDs, we speculate that the inner filter effect (IFE) or fluorescence resonance energy transfer (FRET) may occur. The quenching caused by FRET is due to the energy transfer of the donor molecule to the acceptor molecule. During this energy transfer process, there is not only an overlap between the absorption spectrum of the acceptor molecule and the emission spectrum of the donor molecule, but also a corresponding change in fluorescence lifetime. From Fig. 2D, the fluorescence lifetime of OCDs did not change significantly after the addition of oxTMB, which indicates that the quenching mechanism does not arise from FRET. In addition, as illustrated in Fig. 2A, when oxTMB and OCDs coexisted, the UV absorption spectrum of oxTMB did not change significantly. This observation indicates that no complexes are generated when oxTMB and OCDs are present together, thereby demonstrating that the observed quenching is not caused by static quenching (SQE) (Molaei, 2020). Therefore, we finally concluded that the fluorescence quenching behavior of OCDs comes from the IFE.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Optimization of sensing conditions for OFL
ce:section.ce:para: In order to achieve optimal dual-mode detection performance, the pH, H2O2 concentration, TMB concentration, and FeNi-MOF concentration in the reaction system were optimized. As depicted in Fig. S2A, a stable state of absorbance was reached when the FeNi-MOF concentration increased by 20 μg/mL. This phenomenon may be attributed to the saturation of the enzyme; further increases in enzyme concentration did not lead to the production of more chromogenic substrates. Therefore, a FeNi-MOF concentration of 20 μg/mL was selected for subsequent experiments. Fig. S2B illustrates that at an H2O2 concentration of 6 mM, maximum absorbance is achieved in colorimetric mode. This suggests that at this specific concentration, all enzymes combine with the substrate to generate intermediate products; even with an increase in substrate concentration, there is no corresponding increase in intermediate product formation. Based on this observation, the impact of H2O2 concentration on ratiometric fluorescence was also investigated. As shown in Fig. S2E, the fluorescence ratio F420 nm/F588 nm remained relatively constant within the range of 2–10 mM H2O2 concentrations. Consequently, a final reaction concentration of 6 mM of H2O2 was chosen for subsequent experiments. The pH range of 3–8 was selected for optimization (Fig. S2C). It is evident that the catalytic activity is enhanced under acidic conditions, particularly at pH 4, where the maximum absorbance value is achieved and the quenching effect on OCDs is also the strongest. This may be attributed to the more favorable formation of the reactive intermediate (•OH) in an acidic medium compared to an alkaline environment (Peng, Zhou, Zhang, & Yang, 2022), resulting in more TMB being oxidized to oxTMB. In the ratiometric fluorescence mode process (Fig. S2F), it can be observed that the fluorescence ratio of F420 nm/F588 nm remains relatively stable within the pH range of 3–5 and exhibits an increasing trend when pH is greater than 5. This is due to the fact that -NH2 in FeNi-MOF can form an alkaline salt with stronger fluorescence performance through an acid-base neutralization reaction under higher pH conditions. As the pH value increases, this alkaline salt will produce more. Finally, a pH value of 4 was determined to be the most suitable condition for the final reaction system. In Fig. S2D, the TMB concentration was optimized. It was observed from the figure that when the concentration of TMB reached 8 mM, the absorbance of the reaction system tended to stabilize. This suggests that the oxidation capacity of TMB by •OH generated by FeNi-MOF catalyzed quantitative hydrogen peroxide during the enzymatic reaction is approaching saturation. Even with further increases in TMB concentration, there is no excessive •OH available for oxidation; hence, 8 mM is determined as the optimal TMB concentration. In summary, the most suitable reaction conditions for biosensors are: FeNi-MOF 20 μg/mL, TMB 8 mM, H2O2 6 mM, and pH 4 was the optimal pH.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Orthogonal optimization of the best sensing conditions and results
ce:section.ce:para: In the optimized conditions mentioned above, the ratio of fluorescence remains relatively stable. Moreover, in ratiometric fluorescence detection mode, carbon dots are used as the objects that are quenched by oxTMB, and the addition amount is fixed, so no subsequent optimization is performed. Based on the experimental results mentioned above, four factors-CFeNi-MOF, CH2O2, CTMB, and pH-were selected to determine the range of levels for the aforementioned factors. An L9 (34) orthogonal experiment was designed with these four factors, and absorbance (Abs) was used as the index. The factors and levels in the design of orthogonal test are shown in Table S2. The results of the orthogonal experiment can be found in Table S3. It is evident from the results that A2B2C2D2 represent the best combination of sensing conditions. According to the range analysis, the influence of the four factors on sensing conditions ranks from high to low as D > A > C > B, meaning that pH has a greater impact than CMOF, CTMB, and CH2O2. This is consistent with the optimal sensing conditions obtained by the above optimization: FeNi-MOF at 20 μg/mL, TMB at 8 mM, H2O2 at 6 mM, and a pH level of 4 were determined to be optimal for this study.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Analytical performance of OFL dual-mode detection
ce:section.ce:para: The concentration of OFL was measured under optimal reaction conditions. For the colorimetric detection, a regression equation was established with the absorbance ratio (A/A0) as the ordinate and the logarithm of COFL as the abscissa. The limit of detection expression (LOD) = 3σ/k was used to calculate the minimum detection limit of dual-mode detection (Where σ is the standard deviation of the results of the blank sample after treatment, and k is the slope of the linear regression equation). As revealed in Fig. 3 A, with an increase in COFL concentration, there was a gradual decrease in absorbance at 652 nm within the reaction system. In the illustration of Fig. 3A, the blue color of the solution gradually becomes lighter. It can be observed from Fig. 3B that there is a linear relationship between UV absorption intensity and COFL concentration within this sensing system. The regression equation Y = 0.0645X+0.4216 yielded a correlation coefficient of R2 = 0.9976 and LOD = 1.1 × 10−9 mg/mL (1.1 ng/kg) (S/N = 3). In ratiometric fluorescence mode, after adding OCDs, the ratio F420 nm/F588 nm of the fluorescence intensity of MOF at 420 nm and the fluorescence intensity of OCDs at 588 nm can be obtained using a fluorescence spectrophotometer with an excitation wavelength of 370 nm. Where (F420 nm/F588 nm)0 represents the fluorescence ratio obtained without adding any samples. The ordinate of the regression equation represents the ratio of the standard sample with OFL added to the blank sample (F420 nm/F588 nm)/(F420 nm/F588 nm)0. Fig. 3C displays, the ratio of (F420 nm/F588 nm)/(F420 nm/F588 nm)0 gradually decreases with the increase of COFL. According to Fig. 3C, the ratio of F420 nm/F588 nm gradually decreases with increasing COFL concentration. Illustration in Fig. 3C, under the irradiation of ultraviolet light (365 nm), the fluorescence color gradually changes from blue to orange. In Fig. 3D, the regression equation is Y = 0.1028X+0.0261, with a correlation coefficient of R2 = 0.9982 and LOD = 0.34 × 10−9 mg/mL (0.34 ng/kg) (S/N = 3). As depicted in Table S4, the average recovery rate within the intra-assay was controlled between 98% and 105%, and the coefficient of variation was ≤4.82%, the average recovery rate within the inter-assay was controlled between 99% and 103%, and the coefficient of variation was ≤4.37%. This suggests that dual-mode detection exhibits exceptional accuracy, sensitivity, and reproducibility, making it suitable for the quantitative detection of OFL.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Study on selectivity and anti-interference of biosensors
ce:section.ce:para: In the process of our established dual-mode detection, we selected Cl−, K+, Na+, Mg2+, Pb2+, Histidine (His), Lysine (Lys), glutamic acid (Glu) and glycine (Gly) as interfering substances for detection, which may exist in food. Additionally, we selected some antibacterial drugs for specificity analysis: furazolidone (FZD), sulfapyridine (SPD), ciprofloxacin (CIP), enrofloxacin (ENR), and norfloxacin (NOR). The blank control group during the experiment was Tris-HCl buffer solution (pH = 7.4, 10 mM). Reflect in Fig. 4 , it is clear that the addition of OFL results in a significant decrease in the fluorescence ratio (F420 nm/F588 nm)/(F420 nm/F588 nm)0 and absorbance of the standard sample. This indicates that no noticeable cross-reaction occurred when these substances were present in the sensing system, and there was also no substantial impact of coexisting substances on the sensing system. The results show that the dual-mode biosensor detection system has good anti-interference ability and excellent selectivity.

ce:section.ce:section-title: The application of dual-mode detection in actual samples
ce:section.ce:para: To verify the feasibility and accuracy of the dual-mode application in actual samples, chicken, prawn, and grass carp were selected as specimens. The blank control group consisted of Tris-HCl buffer solution (pH = 7.4, 10 mM), while other samples were added with different COFL standards (10−6, 10−5, 10−4 mg/mL) for detection. In addition, the results were compared with those obtained using commercial enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay kits (Implementation standards: Q/FD 0040 2023 Food Safety ELISA Detection Kit). The results are presented in Table 1 . Among them, in the detection of real samples, the presence of OFL was not detected in both the dual-mode and commercial kits. In the colorimetric/ratiometric fluorescence dual-mode detection process, the recovery range was found to be between 98% and 105% (S/N = 3) with relative standard deviation (RSD)≤4.95%. In comparison, during the commercial kit detection process, the recovery range was between 92 and 103% (S/N = 3) with RSD≤4.91%. These findings demonstrate that the dual mode can be applied to detect actual samples and exhibit outstanding accuracy. Furthermore, the results obtained between the sensing system and the commercial detection kit system are much the same (P > 0.05), demonstrating the applicability of the dual-mode assay to detect antibiotic OFL residues in real samples.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Construction of a detection platform combined with smartphone
ce:section.ce:para: Inspired by these experimental results, a new detection method combined with smartphones is proposed. During the detection process, a smartphone equipped with a self-compiled color recognition application (APP) (Fig. 5 A) is used to convert the colorimetric and fluorescent images into RGB values, which can be used for quantitative detection through simple color changes. Fig. 5B is a standard color card, which can be used as a reference for on-site rapid semi-quantitative detection. Displayed in Fig. 5C, the concentration of OFL increased from 10−7 to 10−2 mg/mL, and the blue color of the sensing system gradually lightened during the colorimetric mode. The G/B value recognized by the smartphone showed a negative correlation with COFL, and the linear regression equation was Y = 0.0263X+0.826, R2 = 0.9976, LOD = 3.52 × 10−9 mg/mL (3.52 ng/kg) (S/N = 3). In ratiometric fluorescence mode detection shown in Fig. 5D, the R/B value of the fluorescence image recognized by the smartphone exhibited a negative correlation with OFL concentration (10−7-10−2 mg/mL), and the linear regression equation was Y = 0.1108X+0.5192, R2 = 0.9982, LOD = 0.26 × 10−9 mg/mL (0.26 ng/kg) (S/N = 3). Overall, compared with other detection methods, smartphone-assisted detection does not require extensive manpower or cumbersome inspection steps. It has its unique advantages and potential in the detection process. To verify the practicability of the smartphone sensing strategy, prawn was used as samples to detect OFL content. As shown in Table S5, it was found that both the recovery and RSD of our method ranged from 96% to 102% and from 1.85% to 4.73%, respectively, indicating that the method had good accuracy and reliability.

Therefore, our two-mode detection platform, which combines a metal-organic skeleton with carbon dots, enables the quantitative detection of OFL in a wide range of concentrations (1 × 10−7-1 × 10−2 mg/mL) and can be applied to real samples. The results of the two tests mutually validate each other, avoiding false positives or false negatives during the detection process, which can lead to misleading results for detection personnel. The antibody-antigen-aptamer sandwich method used in this experiment combines the advantages of both an immunosensor and an aptamer biosensor, demonstrating satisfactory selectivity, sensitivity, and anti-interference capabilities. At the same time, the smartphone-assisted detection method overcomes the limitations of user population and enables convenient, simple, and fast on-site detection. Synthesizing all the above research results, our study provides a reference for the design of colorimetric/fluorescent dual-mode biosensors for quinolones detection in the field of food safety.
